explanation.html_text:
Question / ప్రశ్న

'Change in behaviour' is the predominant aspect in the definition of

'ప్రవర్తనలో మార్పు' అనేది దీని నిర్వచనంలో ప్రధానమైన అంశం

  1. Personalityమూర్తిమత్వం
  2. Aptitudeసహజ సామర్థ్యం
  3. Learningఅభ్యసనం
  4. Interestఅభిరుచి

Correct answer: C

Learning

అభ్యసనం

Why this is right

The most widely accepted definition of Learning (అభ్యసనం) in educational psychology is: "a relatively permanent change in behaviour that occurs as a result of experience or practice." The phrase "change in behaviour" (ప్రవర్తనలో మార్పు) is the defining hallmark of this concept. Behaviourist psychologists such as B.F. Skinner and J.B. Watson centred their entire framework on observable behaviour change as the only measurable evidence that learning has occurred. Even cognitive definitions retain behaviour change as a necessary outcome indicator. No other option in this question places behaviour change at the core of its definition.

Why the others are wrong

A — Personality (మూర్తిమత్వం): Personality is defined as a stable pattern of thoughts, feelings, and behaviours that distinguish a person; its definitions emphasise consistency and individuality, not change.
B — Aptitude (సహజ సామర్థ్యం): Aptitude refers to an innate potential or natural talent to acquire a skill; it is about capacity before learning, not about change produced by experience.
D — Interest (అభిరుచి): Interest is an affective (emotional) orientation towards an activity or subject; its definition centres on preference and attention, not on behaviour change.

Concept notes

Learning (అభ్యసనం) — Key theorists and definitions:
• Behaviourist definition (B.F. Skinner, J.B. Watson, E.L. Thorndike): Learning = relatively permanent change in behaviour due to experience, practice, or conditioning. Stimulus–Response (S–R) bonds are strengthened through reinforcement (బలపరచడం).
• Cognitive definition (Tolman, Piaget, Vygotsky): Learning involves internal mental restructuring; behaviour change is the observable evidence of this internal change.
• Key qualifiers in the standard definition: (1) relatively permanent — excludes fatigue, drugs, maturation; (2) due to experience/practice — excludes reflexes and instincts.
• Contrast with Maturation (పరిపక్వత): Maturation is biological unfolding (e.g., puberty) independent of experience; learning requires experience.
• Types of learning tested in TET: Classical Conditioning (Pavlov), Operant Conditioning (Skinner), Observational/Vicarious Learning (Bandura — Social Learning Theory).
Remember: whenever a TET question mentions "permanent change in behaviour" or "experience-based change," the answer is Learning.

Likely spin-off TET questions

  1. "A relatively permanent change in behaviour as a result of experience is called _____." (Answer: Learning / అభ్యసనం)
  2. "Which psychologist defined learning as 'change in behaviour due to reinforcement'?" (Answer: B.F. Skinner)
  3. "What distinguishes Learning from Maturation?" (Answer: Learning requires experience/practice; maturation is biologically driven and does not require experience.)

Memory hook

"LEARN = Change" — If the question stem says "change in behaviour," the answer is always Learning (అభ్యసనం). Personality is stable, Aptitude is innate capacity, Interest is preference — only Learning is defined by change produced by experience. మార్పు కనిపిస్తే → అభ్యసనం గుర్తుంచుకో!
